behaviors.description: Log entries with the same trace ID are grouped together regardless of which microservice emitted them
behaviors.justification: Grouping is pure data aggregation logic—extract trace ID, bucket by it, collect results. No I/O or coordination between modules. Testing with a list of LogEntry objects from different services (varied service field, overlapping trace IDs) directly validates the grouping algorithm. Real failure (entries dropped, wrong buckets) is caught by deterministic in-process test data.
behaviors.test_description: Create log entries from multiple microservices (A, B, C) with overlapping trace IDs, call group_by_trace_id(), verify each trace ID bucket contains all entries with that ID and no entries are lost.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Unit test with diverse test data fully exercises grouping logic without I/O or concurrency.

behaviors.description: The timeline view orders entries by timestamp, correctly handling clock skew of up to 500ms between services
behaviors.justification: Timestamp ordering and clock-skew correction are deterministic algorithms. With pre-set timestamps (including 500ms offsets simulating clock skew), the ordering function can be fully validated in isolation. Real failure (wrong order despite skew) is caught by deterministic test data without I/O or concurrency.
behaviors.test_description: Create log entries with timestamps 0ms, 250ms, 500ms, 100ms (mixed order, simulating clock skew), pass to ordering function, verify output is sorted by timestamp despite the initial disorder.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Unit test with fixed timestamp values deterministically validates the ordering and skew-compensation algorithm.

behaviors.description: A latency spike (p99 above 3x the baseline) is detected and flagged in the anomaly report
behaviors.justification: Spike detection is a statistical algorithm: compute p99 latency, compare to baseline, flag if exceeds threshold. Fully deterministic. Real failure (spikes not detected, wrong threshold) is caught by unit test with computed latencies and known expected outcomes.
behaviors.test_description: Create log entries with latencies: baseline of 10ms–20ms (normal), then add entries with latencies 60ms+ (spike = 3x baseline), call spike-detection function, verify anomaly report contains the spike flag.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Unit test with computed latency values validates percentile calculation and threshold logic without I/O.

behaviors.description: Malformed JSON log entries are skipped without crashing the pipeline, and the count of skipped entries is reported
behaviors.justification: Error handling is localized to JSON parsing. Testing with actual malformed JSON strings directly exercises the try/except path: real failure (crash, no count increment) is caught by unit test. No I/O or module coordination needed.
behaviors.test_description: Create batch: 8 valid JSON logs + 2 malformed (e.g., missing brace, invalid escape), call ingest(), verify pipeline returns successfully, skipped count == 2, results contains only 8 entries.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Unit test with real malformed JSON data exercises error handling without I/O, catching crash and counter bugs.

behaviors.description: The tool handles a burst of 10,000 log entries per second without dropping entries or falling behind
behaviors.justification: Real failure is dropping entries or falling behind under load—this depends on buffering limits, queue size, concurrency, and processing speed. A unit test with 10k in-memory entries doesn't reveal queue overflow, synchronization bottlenecks, or I/O latency. Mock-miss check: yes, mocking the queue or I/O hides real throughput failures. Integration test with concurrent producers, real buffering (or realistic simulation), and actual processing overhead catches drops and backlog.
behaviors.test_description: Set up test harness with multiple concurrent producers (threads/goroutines) sending log entries into a buffered ingestion queue, a real processing pipeline (JSON parse, group, order), and verified storage. Burst 10,000 entries in rapid succession, measure throughput, verify zero entries dropped and backlog drains without unbounded growth.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration test with concurrent ingestion and real queue behavior detects overflow, processing bottlenecks, and data loss—actual failure modes under load.
